topics.title: TOPIC 1-JOB
topics.description: Office routines, interviews, and team communication.

lessons.title: WORD 1
lessons.description: Listen to short workplace statements and type the complete sentence.

entries.transcript: The new sales manager starts work at nine o'clock every morning.
Please submit the hiring report before the interview begins.
Our team usually meets in the conference room after lunch.
The receptionist will call you when the director is available.
Everyone in the office must wear an identification badge.

lessons.title: WORD 2
lessons.description: Practice longer office sentences with names, numbers, and schedules.

entries.transcript: The applicant has five years of experience in customer service.
We need to schedule another training session for new employees.
His supervisor praised him for finishing the project early.
The accountant is preparing the monthly payroll this afternoon.
Several interns are helping with data entry this week.

topics.title: TOPIC 2-ADVERTISING-MARKETING-PROMOTION
topics.description: Campaign launches, discounts, and brand communication.

lessons.title: WORD 1
lessons.description: Focus on promotions and customer-facing vocabulary.

entries.transcript: The company launched a new advertising campaign on social media.
Our marketing team is designing posters for the summer event.
Customers can receive a discount coupon by email today.
The promotion increased online sales by almost twenty percent.
Please review the brochure before we print one thousand copies.

lessons.title: WORD 2
lessons.description: Train on slogans, target audiences, and media channels.

entries.transcript: The brand manager wants to target college students this season.
We should update the website banner with the new product image.
Many shoppers visited the store after watching the television commercial.
The agency suggested a shorter slogan for the radio advertisement.
Our biggest competitor announced a special offer last weekend.

topics.title: DAY 1
topics.description: Short beginner-friendly dictation for daily listening habits.

lessons.title: WORD 1
lessons.description: Warm up with clear and simple everyday sentences.

entries.transcript: I usually check my email before breakfast.
My class starts at eight fifteen every Monday.
Please bring your notebook to the training room.
We often practice listening in the library after school.
Her teacher speaks slowly during the first lesson.

lessons.title: WORD 2
lessons.description: Continue with polite requests and simple directions.

entries.transcript: Can you open the window near the front desk?
The blue folder is on the chair beside the printer.
I need to finish this homework before dinner tonight.
Our coach gives useful tips for pronunciation practice.
The student in the red jacket asked an easy question.

topics.title: DAY 2
topics.description: Simple listening about schedules, lessons, and study tasks.

lessons.title: WORD 1
lessons.description: Practice dates, time expressions, and classroom actions.

entries.transcript: The afternoon workshop ends at four thirty on Tuesday.
Please write your full name on the top of the page.
They are waiting outside the classroom for the next session.
My partner will send the homework file after lunch.
The audio exercise is easier when you listen twice.

lessons.title: WORD 2
lessons.description: Build confidence with natural but slow daily English.

entries.transcript: We need more pencils for the speaking class tomorrow.
The front door closes automatically after six o'clock.
Her friend borrowed a dictionary from the study center.
This listening test includes ten short sentences and one dialogue.
Please turn off your phone during the practice activity.

topics.title: DAY 3
topics.description: Daily study stories with slightly longer sentence patterns.

lessons.title: WORD 1
lessons.description: Listen for key verbs, adjectives, and classroom vocabulary.

entries.transcript: The teacher explained the answer in a very clear voice.
I forgot my headphones, so I borrowed a pair at the office.
The students reviewed new words before the mock test started.
Please circle the correct answer and check your spelling.
Our class will move to the second floor next week.

lessons.title: WORD 2
lessons.description: More natural sentences to sharpen your typing accuracy.

entries.transcript: She practices English for thirty minutes before going to work.
The center posted the updated timetable on the notice board.
Please save the document in the shared folder on your computer.
My teammate corrected the sentence after listening one more time.
We will take a short break before the final speaking activity.

topics.title: TOPIC 3-MANUFACTURING
topics.description: Factory operations, production plans, and safety updates.

lessons.title: WORD 1
lessons.description: Train on production lines, equipment, and quality checks.

entries.transcript: The factory installed new equipment to improve safety standards.
Workers must inspect each machine before the first shift begins.
Our production manager expects higher output this quarter.
The quality control team rejected several damaged parts yesterday.
Please store the finished goods in warehouse number three.

lessons.title: WORD 2
lessons.description: Practice work orders, materials, and shipping preparation.

entries.transcript: The assembly line stopped because one conveyor belt was broken.
We ordered extra steel materials for next month's large contract.
Every technician should report equipment issues immediately.
The supplier delivered the replacement parts earlier than expected.
Management plans to expand the plant near the river next year.

topics.title: DAY 4
topics.description: Friendly review set before moving to more advanced TOEIC topics.

lessons.title: WORD 1
lessons.description: Review familiar sentence structures with daily vocabulary.

entries.transcript: The teacher asked us to repeat the sentence one more time.
My younger brother studies English in the evening at home.
Please put your bag under the desk before class begins.
The practice room is quieter in the morning than at night.
We will check the answers together after the listening task.

lessons.title: WORD 2
lessons.description: Push accuracy with longer beginner-level sentences.

entries.transcript: She wrote the new vocabulary in a small blue notebook.
Our class manager sent the meeting link before nine o'clock.
The coffee shop near the center opens very early every day.
Please listen carefully and type the sentence exactly as you hear it.
The student corrected two spelling mistakes before submitting the form.

topics.title: TOPIC 4-SHIPPING
topics.description: Logistics, delivery updates, and warehouse coordination.

lessons.title: WORD 1
lessons.description: Focus on deliveries, ports, and warehouse movements.

entries.transcript: The package was delayed because of heavy rain at the port.
Please confirm the delivery address before the truck departs.
Our warehouse staff loaded the boxes onto the second container.
The shipping clerk printed all the customs documents this morning.
Customers can track their orders on the company website.

lessons.title: WORD 2
lessons.description: Train on routes, schedules, and freight communication.

entries.transcript: The express courier will arrive before noon with the replacement parts.
We need to update the freight schedule for next Friday.
A customer complained that one carton was missing from the order.
The logistics coordinator called the driver about a traffic delay.
All export items must be packed securely before inspection.
